title: Read a Nutrition Label
summary: Read a nutrition label and understand serving size, calories, and key nutrients.
learnerPromise: You will be able to read a nutrition label and understand what the numbers mean for one serving.
whyItMatters: Nutrition labels help you compare foods and make choices that match your needs. This is useful when managing energy, allergies, or health goals.
realLifeUses: Comparing two cereal boxes to see which has less added sugar
Checking how much sodium is in a can of soup
Figuring out if one snack bar will keep you full until lunch
Checking serving size to understand how much you are actually eating
materialsNeeded: Two packaged food items with nutrition labels
result: You can explain the serving size, calories, and one key nutrient for each item.
youWillLearn: Locate and read serving size on a label
Spot key nutrients like fat, sodium, sugar, and protein
Compare two similar products using label information
miniChallenge: Pick two food packages from your kitchen and compare their nutrition labels. Which one has more protein per serving? Which has less added sugar?
steps: Find the nutrition label on each package.
Look at the serving size first and picture that amount.
Check the calories per serving on each package.
Look at one or two nutrients that matter to you (for example, sodium, fiber, or added sugar).
Compare the two items and decide which fits your needs better.
completionCriteria: You can state the serving size and calories for each item
You identified one meaningful difference between the two labels
You can explain your choice in one sentence
commonProblems: Ignoring serving size and assuming the whole package is one serving
Getting overwhelmed by all the numbers on the label
Not noticing that added sugar is listed separately from total sugar
tips: Start by looking at just three things: serving size, calories, and one nutrient you care about.
% Daily Value is based on a general adult diet; your needs may be different.
Ingredients are listed from most to least by weight—useful for spotting hidden sugars or salts.
